Health Care Roundup: Market Talk
data.description:
The latest Market Talks covering the Health Care sector. Published exclusively on Dow Jones Newswires at 4:20 ET, 12:20 ET and 16:50 ET.

1153 ET - Praxis Precision Medicines is still a buy despite the late-stage study setback for ulixacaltamide in essential tremor, analysts at Truist say. In a research note, Truist says it still likes Praxis for vormatrigine in focal onset seizures and relutrigine in developmental and epileptic epilepsies, which the firm says puts a floor on the stock. Truist removes essential tremor from its Praxis valuation, which drops its price target on the stock to $85 from $175. Praxis down 40% to $39.26. (colin.kellaher@wsj.com)

2214 ET - IHH Healthcare's long-term prospects may remain optimistic, given its strategic plan for organic and inorganic growth, RHB IB analyst Oong Chun Sung says in a note. The healthcare service provider is targeting 4,000 new beds by 2028, with key expansion focussed on Malaysia and India, he notes. Oong cuts IHH's 2025-2026 earnings forecast by 3% and 1%, respectively, to factor in higher finance costs. RHB cuts IHH Healthcare's target price to MYR8.70 from MYR9.10 as its 2024 earnings came in below estimates, while maintaining a buy rating on the stock. Shares are 1.1% higher at MYR7.39. (yingxian.wong@wsj.com)

1854 ET - Six options appear to be available to Ramsay Health Care as it reviews its 52.8% stake in European private hospital business Ramsay Santé, suggests Citi. It could strike a deal with Predica, the major shareholder in Santé, which has a right of first refusal, or it could find another buyer. Ramsay could consider an in-specie distribution of Santé shares to its own shareholders, or sell the Nordics division to recapitalize Santé. Other options include injecting more equity into Santé, or maintaining the status quo. "We estimate 52.8% of Ramsay Santé could be worth A$2.30-A$4.80 per Ramsay share," analyst Mathieu Chevrier says. Citi has a neutral call on Ramsay. (david.winning@wsj.com; @dwinningWSJ)

1836 ET - Ramsay Health Care is reviewing its 52.8% stake in European private hospital business Ramsay Santé, leading Macquarie to estimate what a deal could look like. Macquarie assumes an Ebitda multiple of 9.0x, citing past transactions and the operating environment right now. Its assumption is ahead of the current trading multiple of 7.5x. "On this basis, we calculate an enterprise value of A$2.7 billion and an equity value of A$1.1 billion, providing a valuation uplift of A$2.00/share or 5% ahead of the current share price," Macquarie says. (david.winning@wsj.com; @dwinningWSJ)

data.title: Financial Services Roundup: Market Talk
data.description:
The latest Market Talks covering Financial Services. Exclusively on Dow Jones Newswires at 4:20 ET, 12:20 ET and 16:50 ET.

1011 ET - In January, just over 41,000 U.S. home-purchase deals, or 14.3% of contracts, fell through, the highest rate for this time of year since 2017 and up from 13.4% last year, Redfin says. Rising supply and falling demand--housing inventory hit a 2020 peak while pending sales dropped to a record low, excluding early in the pandemic--favor buyers, some of whom cancel during inspections for better options. Economic uncertainty, including tariffs and layoffs, plus high mortgage rates--6.96% in January--and a 4.1% rise in median home prices, fueled buyer hesitation. Cancellation rates exceed typical early-year levels but fall short of peaks like 16.4% in March 2020 or late 2022, when mortgage rates soared above 7% for the first time in 20 years. Redfin says buyers should monitor desired homes, as deals collapse post-bidding wars. (chris.wack@wsj.com)

0948 ET - Although Laurentian delivered a 1% miss to consensus in an earnings season that was dominated by large beats from Canada's bigger banks, Scotiabank says Laurentian doesn't have the same type of trading business that boosted larger peers. While the landscape remains challenging in 2025, analyst Meny Grauman says, "overall we have a favorable view of this quarter, although we admit that we lowered our expectation heading into earnings season." Taking a closer look at the key earnings drivers, Grauman says the 9% beat to Scotiabank's estimate on core cash EPS was driven by 8 basis points of margin expansion "which in our view is the key strength of the quarter," Grauman says.(adriano.marchese@wsj.com)

0544 ET - Allianz's 2 billion euro share buyback is a positive surprise and exceeded consensus forecasts, but the company's guidance leaves little room for growth, Jefferies analyst Philip Kett says. The German insurer guided for operating profit of 15 billion euros to 17 billion euros in 2025, compared to consensus at just slightly above 17 billion euros, Kett says in a note. Jefferies adds that though it doesn't expect consensus expectations to decline, it struggles to see incremental gains to earnings per share when consensus estimates are already slightly ahead of the top end of the guided range. Allianz shares trade 0.93% lower to 330.20 euros. (pierre.bertrand@wsj.com)

0441 ET - Hong Kong's financial markets have awoken from their early-2021 slump, Morningstar's Roy Van Keulen writes in a note. Morningstar raises its fair-value estimate on the Hong Kong Exchanges & Clearing stock to HK$325.00 from HK$301.72 following the significant acceleration in 4Q revenue and trading activity, which beat expectations. However, the exchange operator's shares appear to be overvalued, as the current growth rates in trading volumes don't look sustainable, the analyst says. Geopolitical tensions between the U.S. and China are also expected to pose a structural headwind. HKEX shares closed 4.1% lower at HK$350.00. (kimberley.kao@wsj.com)

0414 ET - Hong Kong Exchanges & Clearing is expected to benefit from increasing turnover and a potential revival of capital market activities in China, OCBC says in a research note. OCBC believes HKEX's solid average daily turnover momentum should be sustainable, driven by improving market sentiment and a pick up in IPO activity. HKEX's unique position in connecting mainland Chinese companies with global markets further supports its outlook, OCBC says. Following home appliance giant Midea's listing last year, CATL, the world's largest EV battery maker, plans to list in Hong Kong this year as well. "Despite the recent share price rally, we believe the re-rating will be sustainable alongside with improving ADT," it adds. OCBC maintains a buy call on HKEX, with a fair value estimate of HK$440.00. Shares last ended at HK$380.00. (sherry.qin@wsj.com)

0344 ET - Allianz's guidance for 2025 might trigger some share weakness as it is below consensus expectations, but the company tends to achieve the higher end of its outlook range throughout the year, Deutsche Bank's Rhea Shah and Amalie Zdravkovic say in a research note. Europe's largest insurer said it expects an operating profit of 16 billion euros this year, plus or minus 1 billion euros. The mid-point of this range is below both Deutsche Bank's estimate of 16.5 billion euros and the 16.7 billion euros anticipated by the consensus, the analysts say. Allianz's shares have done well into the results this week, so there could be some weakness due to the company's guidance, Deutsche Bank says. Shares trade 0.5% lower, but are up 3.6% week to date. (adria.calatayud@wsj.com)

2136 ET - RHB Bank's net interest margin may widen in 1Q, amid the easing of seasonal fixed deposit rivalry and strict pricing strategy, Hong Leong IB analyst Chan Jit Hoong says in a note. Loan growth is likely to remain strong, given the resilience in the economy, he reckons. However, net credit cost may slightly rise amid expectations of higher provisions, he says. RHB's risk-reward profile appears attractive, supported by about dividend yield of 6% and an undemanding valuation, he adds. Chan raises RHB's 2025-2026 profit forecasts by 4% each to factor in stronger top-line prospects. Hong Leong also raises RHB's target price to MYR7.80 from MYR7.55, maintaining a buy rating on the stock. Shares are 0.7% higher at MYR6.82. (yingxian.wong@wsj.com)

1905 ET - Medibank Private's policyholder growth was the biggest letdown in its 1H result for Citi, which questions whether the Australian health insurer can improve it. Medibank said net resident policyholder growth was 0.9% in 1H, well shy of its target of growing in line with system, analyst Nigel Pittaway says. Medibank's new goal is to grow in line with the market in 2H, and expand its share of the market in FY 2026. "Given its recent growth struggles, we remain a little skeptical of this," Citi says. Still, Citi says Medibank's decision to return another A$160 million to customers as part of its Covid-19 support package should help. (david.winning@wsj.com; @dwinningWSJ)

data.title: Energy & Utilities Roundup: Market Talk
data.description:
The latest Market Talks covering Energy and Utilities. Published exclusively on Dow Jones Newswires at 4:20 ET, 12:20 ET and 16:50 ET.

1522 ET - Oil futures finish the week lower after trading in a tight range, with the market weighing uncertainty around the impact of imminent U.S. tariffs on Canada and Mexico, and the likelihood of OPEC and its allies keeping output cuts in place beyond March. On the bullish side were the U.S. decision to revoke the license under which Chevron produces oil in Venezuela, and a unexpected drop in U.S. crude oil stocks. WTI settles at $69.76, down 0.9% on the week. Brent for April delivery goes off the board at $73.18 a barrel, down 1.7% from a week ago.(anthony.harrup@wsj.com)

1407 ET - New figures indicate Canadian energy production hit record levels in December. The report, issued by Statistics Canada, was overlooked due to the release of surprisingly strong 4Q GDP data. The data agency says crude-oil production in Canada rose 2% in December from a year ago to 27.1 million cubic meters, or a record. Exports of crude oil and equivalents also reached a record in December, up 2.1% from a year ago, driven primarily by shipments to the US via rail and tanker ship. Natural-gas output also reached a record. President Trump is threatening to impose a 10% tariff on Canadian imports of energy products starting March 4. Data indicate that 95% of Canada's crude-oil exports were US-bound. (Paul.Vieira@wsj.com, @paulvieira)

1328 ET - The number of rigs drilling for oil in the U.S. fell by two this week to 486, a first decline in five weeks, and was down by 20 from a year ago, oil services company Baker Hughes reports. Rigs drilling for natural gas increased by three to 102, or 17 fewer than a year ago. Top U.S. natural gas producer Expand Energy said this week it expects to run about 12 rigs in 2025 with average production estimated at 7.1 billion cubic feet equivalent a day. The company plans to add capacity in the second half, running around 15 rigs, and end the year at 7.2 Bcfe/d, raising that to an average of 7.5 Bcfe/d in 2026 "should market conditions warrant." (anthony.harrup@wsj.com)

1003 ET - Crude's gains on the U.S. decision to revoke a license under which Chevron produces oil in Venezuela prove fleeting, with futures back in the red as the market awaits more clarity on U.S. import tariffs and whether or not OPEC+ will again extend the timeline for returning barrels to the market. "Oil prices are fluctuating within a trading range, but a delay will give prices an upside breakout," Phil Flynn of the Price Futures Group says in a note. WTI is off 1.5% and trading back below $70 a barrel, and Brent is down 1.4% at $73.02 with the April contract expiring at the close. (anthony.harrup@wsj.com)

0827 ET - Eni remains committed to energy transition-related investments as its oil and gas peers withdraw from them, HSBC analysts write. The Italian oil major's 2025 to 28 strategy doubles down on biorefining and renewable assets, they write. It's new strategy contained no major changes, they write. This is somewhat remarkable given peers BP, Equinor and Shell have all recently scaled back their climate ambitions and investments in low-carbon assets, they write. Shares trade down 1.6% at 13.85 euros.(adam.whittaker@wsj.com)

0815 ET - Eni's share buyback could grow to 2.5 billion euros this year, Jefferies analysts Giacomo Romeo and Kai Ye Loh write. The Italian energy major plans to initially buy back 1.5 billion euros of shares over the year but said it could be as high as 3.5 billion euros. In reality, it is more likely to be between 1.8 billion and 2.5 billion euros, the analysts write. Overall, the oil major's revised strategy contains higher-than-expected distributions, higher upstream production and unchanged capital expenditure guidance despite exchange-rate headwinds, the analysts write. Shares trade down 1.6% at 13.85 euros. (adam.whittaker@wsj.com)

0751 ET - Eni's updated strategy contained no surprises and effectively extends prior strategic guidance, RBC Capital Markets analysts Biraj Borkhataria and Adnan Dhanani write. The strategy, set out Thursday, continues to focus on value creation in its upstream division and a focused energy transition strategy which is already working, they write. The Italian energy major has continued its joint-venture 'satellite' model but as this proliferates, investors may begin to wonder whether the benefits are offset by the complicated nature of its portfolio, they add. "Understanding cash flow trends over time will depend on the dividend policies of such entities, and this creates less transparency," they write. Shares trade down 1.6% at 13.85 euros. (adam.whittaker@wsj.com)

0452 ET - Orsted's capital expenditure cuts fundamentally change the business and should mean that between 2027 and 2030 its capex falls to just 20 billion Danish kroner a year, UBS analyst Mark Freshney writes. "At this point, sources of cash should exceed uses, even with the dividend restored to 12 kroner a share." The bulk of Orsted's 2024-2030 capex plan will be seen in 2025 and 2026, after which net debt steadily falls and Ebitda reaches around 40 billion kroner a year, he adds. Competition has dried up and UBS thinks parts of the supply chain will become more accessible through 2025 and 2026. UBS lowers its price target to 420 kroner from 460 kroner and keeps at buy. Shares trade 0.1% lower at 314.60 kroner. (dominic.chopping@wsj.com)

2146 ET - Sembcorp Industries looks well-positioned to capture various opportunities, OCBC Investment Research says in a note. These opportunities include energy transition, greater artificial intelligence usage and industrial realignment, the OCBC team says. Going forward, the Singapore-listed energy, water and urban development group's renewables segment is likely to grow, the team says. Drivers are full-year contribution of assets acquired during the year and commissioning of greenfield projects, it adds. OCBC raises the stock's fair value estimate to S$7.20 from S$6.70 with an unchanged buy rating. Shares are 0.8% higher at S$6.08. (ronnie.harui@wsj.com)

1831 ET - Barrenjoey's verdict on Karoon Energy's US$115 million deal to buy the floating production storage and offloading vessel at its Bauna oil field in Brazil: it's a steal. Analyst Dale Koenders previously estimated a deal would be done for US$150 million. The acquisition will enable Karoon to save US$25 million-US$45 million of annual net costs and better manage maintenance to improve reliability. It also means Karoon can be sure of an FPSO being available when its current lease ends, enabling it to investigate extending Bauna's life. "We estimate an internal rate of return on acquisition of 23%, and value of A$0.20/share," Barrenjoey says, although this was already assumed in its stock valuation. (david.winning@wsj.com; @dwinningWSJ)

data.title: Basic Materials Roundup: Market Talk
data.description:
The latest Market Talks covering Basic Materials. Published exclusively on Dow Jones Newswires at 4:20 ET, 12:20 ET and 16:50 ET.

1430 ET - Front-month gold futures had a tough week, dropping 3.5% amid heightened concerns about the U.S. officially imposing a 25% tariff on Canada and Mexico, as well as an additional tariff of China. It's the largest weekly decline since November 2024. Adding to the slide was today's drop of 1.6%, to $2,836.80 a troy ounce. Fundamentally, uncertainty in the U.S. economy stemming from the tariff situation should support gold, says Taylor Krystkowiak of Themes ETF. But fund traders have been pulling out from the sizable long positions they hold, says Krystkowiak. "Gold will likely need to consolidate again before it gains the momentum needed to break through the psychological resistance level of $3,000/oz," he says. Gold finished February up 0.9%. (kirk.maltais@wsj.com)

0828 ET - BASF's 2025 outlook is similar to last year and reflects low upstream margins, UBS analysts say in a note. Excluding the start-up costs for its China site, the midpoint of its Ebitda guidance would mean a 10% or more on-year increase, they say. The German chemical giant estimates double-digit Ebitda growth for its Surface Technology segment in 2025, which is surprising given its high exposure to a weakened automotive sector, the analysts add. Its 4Q results showed a weak performance of its core segments, significantly below market expectations except for its Nutrition & Care business, they say. Shares are up 0.9% at 49.21 euros. (helena.smolak@wsj.com)

0514 ET - Palm oil ended higher amid tight supply. Despite recent supply concerns, palm oil inventory at key producing countries is likely to reach a balance in March and April, and may start to accumulate after May if palm oil remains at high prices, Minmetals Futures said in a research note. Global consumption of vegetable oils in 2025 also remained uncertain after Malaysia and Brazil delayed raising their mandatory biodiesel blend rates. Meanwhile, cargo surveyor AmSpec Agri Malaysia estimated that Malaysia's palm oil exports during the Feb. 1-28 period dropped 8.51% on month. The Bursa Malaysia Derivatives contract for May delivery rose 42 ringgit at 4,553 ringgit a ton. (sherry.qin@wsj.com)

0211 ET - BASF's 2024 results will likely cause a muted share-price reaction, Jefferies analysts say in a note. The German chemical giant expects for 2025 an adjusted Ebitda of between 8.0 billion euros and 8.4 billion euros, slightly below consensus of 8.4 billion euros, they say. Divisionally, the company's agricultural and other segments were above Jefferies' consensus estimates, whereas chemicals and materials came in below expectations, they add. (helena.smolak@wsj.com)

2219 ET - SD Guthrie's 2025 earnings outlook remains strong, driven by firm palm oil prices and improving upstream performance, Kenanga IB analyst Khoo Teng Chuan says in a note. CPO prices are expected to average MYR4,200/ton in 2025, higher than the previously expected MYR4,000/ton. Better fruit yields and lower fertilizer costs should help contain unit costs despite wage inflation, he says. While downstream demand may gradually recover, margins are expected to remain tight, he says. SD Guthrie is also expanding into renewable energy and industrial property developments, with contributions expected from 2026, he notes. Khoo raises SD Guthrie's 2025 core EPS forecasts by 5% due to higher CPO prices estimates. Kenanga raises the target price to MYR4.60 from MYR4.65, maintaining a market perform rating on the stock. Shares are 1.2% higher at MYR5.03. (yingxian.wong@wsj.com)

2142 ET - Palm oil prices fall in Asia's trading session, tracking weakness in soybean oil on the Chicago Board of Trade overnight, Kenanga Futures says in a note. Concerns over palm oil's premium compared to its rival, soy oil, could weigh on demand, it says. Expectations of higher production in 2H is also seen as pressuring palm oil prices. It adds that short-covering ahead of the weekend is likely to provide some support. Kenanga Futures pegs CPO futures support at 4,400 ringgit/ton and resistance at 4,550 ringgit/ton. The Bursa Malaysia Derivatives contract for May delivery falls 9 ringgit to 4,502 ringgit/ton. (yingxian.wong@wsj.com)

2132 ET - Rio Tinto says it isn't in the interest of its shareholders to divulge its tax exposures via an independent review of its dual-listed structure, as requested by Palliser Capital. Macquarie agrees there is a risk in airing the full details publicly. "Public examination raises the risk of renegotiation," Macquarie analysts say in a note. Rio Tinto has rejected calls by Palliser to drop its dual listing, claiming tax costs in the mid-single-digit billions of dollars. It hasn't released fuller details of its own internal review. "If aired, then the risk may eventuate regardless, changing the incremental friction costs," Macquarie says. Still, the Australian bank reckons there are potential benefits to unification, which could include improve valuation for London investors and accelerate the use of Australian tax, or franking, credits. (rhiannon.hoyle@wsj.com; @RhiannonHoyle)

2113 ET - Iron-ore prices are likely to average around $100 a metric ton this year, as subdued Chinese demand, stable supplies and elevated inventories cap prices, according to BMI, a unit of Fitch Solutions. "Positive sentiment bolstered by tentative signs of improving fundamentals has counterbalanced fresh tariff threats," both on U.S. steel imports and Chinese goods more broadly, BMI says. Still, China's recent high level of imports are mostly swelling stockpiles, and its prolonged housing-market downturn will continue to act as a drag on its steel sector, BMI says. Spot iron ore fell 0.9% Thursday to $105.85/ton, according to S&P Global Commodity Insights. (rhiannon.hoyle@wsj.com; @RhiannonHoyle)

data.title: Auto & Transport Roundup: Market Talk
data.description:
The latest Market Talks covering the Auto and Transport sector. Published exclusively on Dow Jones Newswires at 4:20 ET, 12:20 ET and 16:50 ET.

0616 ET - IAG's fourth-quarter performance was driven by stronger-than-expected revenue, somewhat offset by higher costs, Goodbody analyst Dudley Shanley says in a research note. Quarterly revenue growth of 11% to 8.05 billion euros compares with Goodbody's forecast of 7.60 billion euros, with the beat largely driven by passenger revenues, which were 4% higher than forecast, the analyst says. "Despite the very strong run the stock had last year and at the start of this year, we think the market will welcome the comments about sustainable earnings growth and the new EUR1B buyback," Shanley says. IAG shares trade up 5% at 3.55 pounds. (pierre.bertrand@wsj.com)

0524 ET - IAG's margins are higher now than before the coronavirus pandemic and they can continue to grow as the airline group's long-haul capacity recovers, Jefferies analysts say in a research note. Its strong balance sheet also gives the British Airways owner the option to continue to add brands to its group, which also includes Iberia and Vueling, or to grow dividends, Jefferies says. Company guidance of around 3% higher passenger-carrying capacity this year with continued strong customer demand implies EBIT consensus at 4.6 billion euros. Shares trade 3.6% higher at 351.0 pence. (pierre.bertrand@wsj.com)

0450 ET - IAG shares should continue to outperform this year as the market underappreciates the group's pricing and earnings power, JPMorgan analysts say in a research note. The British Airways, Iberia and Vueling owner is the brokerage's top pick for 2025. The analysts expect consensus expectations for earnings this year to tick upward by at least a few percentages, they say. Among the positive takeaways from the company's year-end result is a 14% increase in transatlantic pricing in 4Q and a new 1 billion euro share-buyback plan which exceeds JPM's expectations. Shares trade up 4.3% at 3.53 pounds. (pierre.bertrand@wsj.com)

0428 ET - IAG's operating profit before exceptional items exceeded analysts' expectations and is expected to grow further this year, RBC Capital Markets analyst Ruairi Cullinane says in a research note. The metric, which strips out exceptional and other one-off items, rose 27% to 4.44 billion euros compared with expectations of 4.08 billion euros. The airline group has a stronger outlook than it is given credit for considering its supportive capacity backdrop on the Atlantic route, pricing potential from British Airways product measures and the possibility for further business travel recovery, RBC says. Shares trade up 5% at 355.70 pence. (pierre.bertrand@wsj.com)

0414 ET - British Airways owner IAG appears to be flying high and with confidence, Interactive Investor's Richard Hunter says in a note to clients. The airline group's buyback of up to 1 billion euros comes as full-year cash flow surged to 3.56 billion euros from 1.32 billion euros. Revenue growth was supported by a 9.5% bump in passenger sales and a 6.7% rise in cargo sales. "Despite the obvious economic challenges which many consumers are currently facing, the annual holiday seems to have become shielded from day-to-day financial constraint," Hunter says. Shares trade up 4.5% at 354.0 pence. (pierre.bertrand@wsj.com)

0404 ET - Valeo's order cancellations are a negative aspect in a set of results that was largely as expected and in which the French auto-parts supplier forecast an earnings improvement this year thanks to its restructuring, Deutsche Bank's Christoph Laskawi says in a note. The company said customers canceled orders valued at around 7.3 billion euros--or 10% of its intake for 2022 and 2023--due to an unsettled economic and technological environment. Peers have likely experienced the same issue, Laskawi says. However, Valeo issued guidance for 2025 that was a touch higher than expected mainly thanks to cost savings and benefits from a restructuring, the analyst says. Shares plummet 12% in their biggest one-day percentage drop in nearly five years. (adria.calatayud@wsj.com)

0350 ET - IAG is benefiting from cheaper fuel prices and resilient demand for travel, which helped boost its operating profit and its share price to five-year highs, Begbies Traynor partner Julie Palmer says in a note to clients. IAG appears to be defying the gloom weighing on consumer-facing sectors in the U.K., the analyst says. The owner of British Airways, Iberia and Vueling posted an operating profit before exceptional items of 4.44 billion euros last year. "Despite current successes, IAG must remain wary of the potential headwinds of a volatile geopolitical climate, the possible impact of tariffs and a highly competitive travel market," the analyst says. IAG shares trade up 3.9% at 351.90 pence. (pierre.bertrand@wsj.com)

0322 ET - Volvo's move to re-hire workers at its largest plant in Europe probably confirms a recovery of the market, Deutsche Bank analyst Nicolai Kempf writes. In North America, the bank understands that the company will continue to build its new plant in Mexico, despite potential tariffs risks. "Overall, we remain constructive on the name, and given a potential recovery of the end markets we increase our target multiples and lift our target price to 360 Swedish kronor from 310 kronor." The bank reiterates its buy rating. Shares trade 1.4% lower at 334.50 kronor. (dominic.chopping@wsj.com)

0317 ET - Forvia hasn't proposed a dividend for 2024 to prioritize debt reduction, as anticipated, but its guidance for 2025 looks weaker than expected, Bernstein analysts say. "The mid-point of Forvia's 2025 outlook is below the current consensus expectations on revenue and operating profit which we think will be taken negatively, especially in the context of [peer] Valeo's results and more upbeat outlook last night," the analysts say. The French auto-parts supplier expects 2025 revenue to be between 26.3 billion euros and 27.5 billion euros and guides for an operating margin of between 5.2% and 6.0%. Its results for last year were broadly in line with consensus expectations, and cash flow surprised positively, Bernstein says. Shares fall 17%. (adria.calatayud@wsj.com)

0244 ET - Citi opens a positive catalyst watch on Volkswagen ahead of the EU's auto action plan announcement on March 5 and the company's March 11 full-year results. Analysts Harald C Hendrikse and Soumava Banerjee say the EU auto strategic action plan could result in VW EPS upgrades if carbon dioxide penalties are diluted. The bank says that while tariff risk remains real it is smaller relative to group EBIT for VW than other German peers. Citi thinks VW's full-year 2025 guidance will take away some of the worst downside risks, while its German restructuring protects earnings downside risks in the midterm. "We think VW shares will continue to run towards our 125 euro target price." Shares closed at 107.20 euros. (dominic.chopping@wsj.com)

2232 ET - Hong Leong Asia's earnings are likely on an upswing from higher deliveries across all business segments, DBS Group Research analysts say in a research report. Its ongoing focus on research and development for the powertrain solutions business and expansion of production to serve data centers and semiconductor industries should continue to spur engine sales volume, the analysts say. With a growing order book and increased focus on cost savings, its building materials segment is expected to continue driving earnings as more public and private sector projects are launched in Singapore. DBS raises the stock's target price to S$1.60 from S$1.28 with an unchanged buy rating. Shares are unchanged at S$0.925. (ronnie.harui@wsj.com)

data.title: Equities Climb as Markets Track Trump-Zelenskyy Clash, Consumer Spending Data
data.description:
US benchmark equity indexes rose on Friday after President Donald Trump's meeting with his Ukrainian counterpart, Volodymyr Zelenskyy, ended without a minerals deal being signed.

The Nasdaq Composite and S&P 500 climbed 1.6% each to 18,847.3 and 5,954.50, respectively. The Dow Jones Industrial Average increased 1.4% at 43,840.9. All sectors registered gains, led by financials.

Earlier in the day, Zelenskyy left the White House after a heated argument between the two sides. The US wants access to Ukraine's rare earth minerals as part of a broader effort to end the conflict between Kyiv and Russia.

US Treasury yields fell, with the two-year rate sinking 9.1 basis points to 3.99% and the 10-year rate dropping 7.7 basis points to 4.21%.

In economic news, US personal consumption expenditures decreased 0.2% in January following a 0.8% gain the month before, the Bureau of Economic Analysis said. A Bloomberg-polled consensus called for a slowdown in spending growth to 0.2%.

The Federal Reserve's preferred core measure, which excludes food and energy, decelerated to 2.6% in January from 2.9% the month before. Sequentially, the core measure accelerated to a 0.3% gain. Both figures were in line with analysts' expectations.

"Broadly speaking, US households remain in good financial shape, supported by a still-healthy labor market and a significant wealth cushion," TD Economics said. "As long as this remains the case, we expect real consumer spending to stay resilient this year, with growth moderating to around 2%."

In company news, NetApp saw the steepest decline on the S&P 500, down 16%. The company late Thursday lowered its fiscal 2025 outlook.

Dell Technologies shares fell 4.7% Friday. The computer maker late Thursday logged stronger-than-expected fiscal fourth-quarter earnings, though its revenue fell short of Wall Street's estimates.

AES shares were up nearly 12%, the best performer on the S&P 500, as the company outlined an upbeat full-year earnings outlook following a quarterly beat.

Monster Beverage was among the top gainers on the S&P 500, up 5.3%. The energy drink company late Thursday posted fourth-quarter sales above market expectations, driven by higher prices and growing demand in the US.

West Texas Intermediate crude oil was down 0.4% at $70.07 a barrel.

data.title: Spirit AeroSystems Holdings, Inc. SEC 10-K Report
data.description:
Spirit AeroSystems Holdings, Inc., a leading manufacturer of aerostructures for commercial and defense aerospace programs, has released its 2024 10-K report. The report provides a comprehensive overview of the company's financial performance, business operations, strategic initiatives, and the challenges it faces in the current market environment.

Financial Highlights

Net Revenues: $6,316.6 million, an increase of 4.4% compared to the prior year, driven by increased production on the B777, A350, and A320 programs.

Gross Loss: ($1,372.4) million, compared to a gross profit of $206.2 million in the prior year, primarily due to greater forward loss charges on the B787, B767, A350, and A220 programs.

Operating Loss: ($1,786.1) million, an increase in loss of $1,651.9 million compared to the prior year, driven by higher unfavorable changes in estimates and increased SG&A expenses related to the merger.

Net Loss: ($2,139.0) million, compared to a net loss of $616.1 million in the prior year, reflecting the impact of increased forward loss charges and higher interest expenses.

Net Loss Attributable to Common Shareholders: ($2,139.8) million, reflecting the overall financial challenges faced during the year.

Business Highlights

Revenue Segments: The company operates in three principal segments: Commercial, Defense & Space, and Aftermarket. The Commercial segment accounted for 78% of net revenues in 2024, with key programs including B737, B767, B777, B787, A220, A320 family, A330, and A350 XWB. The Defense & Space segment contributed 15% of net revenues, with programs such as the B767 Tanker, P-8, and Sikorsky CH-53K. The Aftermarket segment made up 7% of net revenues, focusing on MRO services and spare parts.

Geographical Performance: The company generated $1,505.6 million in sales to international customers in 2024, primarily to Airbus, with significant contributions from the U.K. and other countries outside the U.S. Approximately 18% of long-lived assets are based in the U.K., with an additional 5% in other international locations.

Sales Units: In 2024, the company delivered 1,432 shipsets, including 376 for Boeing and 825 for Airbus. Notable deliveries included 268 B737, 55 B787, 648 A320 family, and 82 A220 units.

New Product Launches: The company continued to focus on developing new technologies and products, particularly in the Defense & Space segment, with ongoing work on classified military programs and the B-21 Raider development.

New Production Launches: The company maintains state-of-the-art manufacturing facilities across multiple locations, including Wichita, Kansas; Tulsa, Oklahoma; and Belfast, Northern Ireland, with ongoing investments to support and maintain industry-leading manufacturing expertise.

Future Outlook: Management's focus for 2025 includes improving quality, safety, and compliance, enhancing operational execution, and achieving cost reductions. The company is also preparing for strategic transactions, including a potential merger with Boeing and divestiture of certain Airbus-related businesses.

Strategic Initiatives

Merger Agreement: The company entered into a Merger Agreement with The Boeing Company, which includes provisions for the divestiture of certain business segments to Airbus SE. This strategic move is aimed at aligning with Boeing's operations and enhancing the company's market position.

Capital Management: The company received significant customer advances, including $100 million from Airbus for the A350 program and $425 million from Boeing to support liquidity. These advances are structured to be repaid through future deliveries. The company also engaged in debt refinancing activities, including the issuance of Exchangeable Senior Notes and a Bridge Credit Agreement, to manage its capital structure effectively.

Future Outlook: The company anticipates the completion of the merger with Boeing by mid-2025, subject to regulatory approvals and other conditions. It plans to continue focusing on liquidity improvement through strategic divestitures and customer advances. The company is also exploring additional funding options to sustain operations, given the ongoing challenges in production and delivery processes.

Challenges and Risks

Dependency on B737 Program: The company faces significant risks related to its dependency on the B737 aircraft program, which has experienced production rate reductions due to the B737 MAX groundings and the COVID-19 pandemic. This dependency creates financial and disruption risks, especially if further suspensions or rate changes occur.

Reliance on Major Customers: The company is heavily reliant on Boeing and Airbus as major customers, and any negative actions or business difficulties they experience could materially affect the company's operations.

Financial Instability: The company has incurred substantial operating losses in recent years, raising concerns about its ability to continue as a going concern. This financial instability is compounded by high levels of debt and increased borrowing costs due to credit rating downgrades. The company's liquidity is further strained by higher inventory levels and operational cash flow challenges.

Operational Risks: Maintaining a healthy supply chain and meeting production rate requirements are significant challenges. The company has faced disruptions due to supplier issues, inflationary pressures, and challenges in hiring skilled labor. Cybersecurity threats also pose significant risks, with potential impacts on manufacturing processes and data integrity.

Regulatory Risks: The company's global operations expose it to changes in international trade policies, tariffs, and compliance with various environmental and safety regulations. The transition to a low-carbon economy presents additional challenges, requiring significant investments and potential changes in product offerings.

Market Risks: The company is exposed to market risks such as fluctuations in raw material prices, particularly for aluminum, titanium, and composite materials. These fluctuations can significantly impact production costs and profitability, especially when contractual protections against inflation are inadequate. Additionally, geopolitical tensions, such as the conflicts in Ukraine and the Middle East, may disrupt supply chains and increase costs.

data.title: P10, Inc. SEC 10-K Report
data.description:
P10, Inc., a leading provider of private market solutions, has released its annual Form 10-K report for the fiscal year ending December 31, 2024. The report highlights the company's robust financial performance, strategic initiatives, and the challenges it faces in the competitive asset management industry. P10, Inc. operates through various segments, including Private Equity Solutions, Venture Capital Solutions, and Private Credit Solutions, with a significant presence in North America and an expanding footprint in Europe.

Financial Highlights

P10, Inc. reported strong financial results for the fiscal year 2024, showcasing significant growth and improved profitability:

  • Total Revenues: $296.4 million, reflecting a 23% increase from the previous year due to higher management and advisory fees and an increase in catch-up fees.
  • Income from Operations: $60.6 million, a significant increase of 190% compared to the previous year, driven by higher revenues and controlled operating expenses.
  • Net Income: $19.7 million, compared to a net loss of $7.8 million in the previous year, indicating a strong recovery and improved profitability.
  • Basic Earnings/(Loss) Per Share: $0.17, showing a positive turnaround from a loss per share of $0.06 in the previous year.
  • Diluted Earnings/(Loss) Per Share: $0.16, reflecting the company's improved financial performance and profitability.

Business Highlights

P10, Inc. demonstrated strong operational performance across its various business segments:

  • Private Equity Solutions (PES): The PES segment focuses on direct and indirect investments in middle and lower-middle market private equity across North America and Europe. As of December 31, 2024, PES managed $14.1 billion of Fee-Paying Assets Under Management (FPAUM). The segment is supported by a team of 42 investment professionals with deep industry relationships.
  • Venture Capital Solutions (VCS): VCS specializes in investments in venture capital funds across North America, targeting high-performing, access-constrained opportunities. As of December 31, 2024, VCS managed $6.4 billion of FPAUM. The segment benefits from strategic partnerships and a strong presence in the venture capital and technology ecosystems.
  • Private Credit Solutions (PCS): PCS primarily makes debt investments targeting lower middle market companies in North America. As of December 31, 2024, PCS managed approximately $5.2 billion of FPAUM. The segment is characterized by a relationship-driven sourcing approach and includes investments in renewable energy and underserved communities.
  • Geographical Performance: The company has a significant presence in North America, where the majority of its capital is deployed. The acquisition of Qualitas Equity Funds SGEIC, S.A. is expected to expand the company's presence in Europe, enhancing its global reach.
  • New Acquisitions: In September 2024, the company entered into an agreement to acquire Qualitas Equity Funds SGEIC, S.A., a European lower middle market private equity fund-of-funds manager. This acquisition is expected to close in the first quarter of 2025 and will establish a European presence for the company.
  • Future Outlook: The company plans to expand within other asset classes and geographies through additional acquisitions and organic growth. It aims to leverage its scale and relationships to enhance its position in the private markets ecosystem.
  • Investment Process: The company maintains a rigorous investment process, leveraging a comprehensive database to track thousands of potential investment opportunities. This process includes initial screening, due diligence, and risk management to ensure optimal investment decisions.
  • Responsible Investment Philosophy: The company integrates environmental, social, and governance (ESG) considerations into its investment process, with certain subsidiaries being signatories to the United Nations Principles for Responsible Investment.
  • Human Capital: As of December 31, 2024, the company employed 267 full-time equivalent employees, including 112 investment professionals. The company emphasizes diversity and inclusion, offering competitive benefits and focusing on talent management to support its growth.

Strategic Initiatives

P10, Inc. has undertaken several strategic initiatives to enhance its market position and drive long-term growth:

  • Expansion of Private Market Solutions: The company has focused on expanding its private market solutions by acquiring Western Technology Investment Advisors LLC (WTI) and restructuring its subsidiaries to enhance operational efficiency. The formation of P10 Advisors to manage investment opportunities that do not fit within existing mandates indicates a strategic move to broaden its investment capabilities.
  • Capital Management: The company has actively managed its capital through a share repurchase program, with $88.5 million spent on buybacks as of December 31, 2024, and an additional $40 million authorized for future repurchases. P10, Inc. has also refinanced its debt, entering into a new senior secured revolving credit facility and a new senior secured loan facility totaling $500 million to replace existing credit facilities and support general corporate purposes, including acquisitions. The company has maintained a disciplined approach to managing its debt obligations, with a focus on maintaining compliance with financial covenants.
  • Future Outlook: P10, Inc. plans to continue its growth strategy by leveraging its scalable business model to expand its asset class solutions and geographic reach. The company aims to capitalize on the increasing demand for private market solutions and intends to explore further acquisitions to enhance its investment platform. Additionally, P10, Inc. is committed to maintaining its data advantage and strengthening its relationships with top private equity fund managers to sustain its competitive position.

Challenges and Risks

P10, Inc. faces several significant risks that could impact its business operations and financial performance:

  • Revenue Dependency and Client Retention: The company's revenue is heavily dependent on management and advisory fees from fee-paying clients. The potential for early withdrawal of committed capital or termination of advisory roles poses a risk to revenue stability. Additionally, the competitive environment for customized separate accounts and advisory contracts could lead to client loss due to mergers, management changes, or competition.
  • Investment Performance and Capital Raising: Poor performance of specialized investment vehicles could hinder the company's ability to raise capital for future funds and attract new clients. Historical performance is not indicative of future results, and market conditions may not be as favorable as in the past.
  • Competition for Investment Opportunities: The company faces intense competition from other asset management firms, banks, and financial institutions for investment opportunities. This competition could limit access to top-performing funds and co-investment opportunities, affecting the company's ability to secure desirable investments.
  • Conflicts of Interest: As the company expands, potential conflicts of interest may arise, particularly in recommending investments in funds managed by its investment management business. Failure to manage these conflicts appropriately could damage the company's reputation and affect its ability to raise funds.
  • Talent Retention: The company's success is reliant on retaining its senior leadership team and attracting qualified professionals. The competitive market for investment professionals poses a risk to maintaining the necessary talent to execute investment strategies effectively.
  • Expansion Risks: The company's strategy to expand into new lines of business and geographic markets, including Europe and Asia, introduces additional risks and uncertainties. These include increased operational costs, regulatory compliance challenges, and potential integration issues with acquisitions.
  • Regulatory and Compliance Risks: The company is subject to extensive regulation, and any failure to comply with applicable laws could result in fines, sanctions, or reputational damage. Changes in regulatory requirements, particularly in the U.S. and EU, could increase compliance costs and impact business operations.
  • Cybersecurity and Data Privacy: The company relies on information technology systems and third-party service providers, making it vulnerable to data breaches and cyberattacks. Such incidents could disrupt operations, compromise sensitive information, and result in financial and reputational harm.
  • Market and Economic Conditions: Adverse market conditions, such as rising interest rates, inflation, and global economic downturns, could negatively impact the company's investment performance and ability to raise capital. These conditions may also affect the liquidity and valuation of investments.
  • Debt and Leverage Risks: The company's use of leverage exposes it to risks associated with debt financing, including increased costs and restrictive covenants that may limit operational flexibility. Defaults by investors in specialized funds could also impact fund operations and performance.

Management's strategies to address these challenges include maintaining strong relationships with fund managers, enhancing risk management procedures, and focusing on talent retention and development. The company is also exploring opportunities to leverage emerging technologies, such as artificial intelligence, to improve operational efficiency and competitive positioning.

data.title: Aldeyra Therapeutics, Inc. SEC 10-K Report
data.description:
Aldeyra Therapeutics, Inc., a biotechnology company focused on developing innovative therapies for immune-mediated and metabolic diseases, has released its 2024 10-K report. The report provides a comprehensive overview of the company's financial performance, business operations, strategic initiatives, and the challenges it faces as it continues to advance its product pipeline and expand its RASP modulator platform.

Financial Highlights

  • Net Loss: $55.9 million, an increase of 48.8% compared to the previous year, primarily due to higher research and development expenses.
  • Loss from Operations: $60.1 million, up by 40.5% year-over-year, driven by increased costs in clinical trials and drug manufacturing.
  • Research and Development Expenses: $48.2 million, a rise of 63.7% due to increased external clinical development and drug product manufacturing costs.
  • General and Administrative Expenses: $11.9 million, a decrease of 10.8%, primarily due to reduced consulting and legal expenditures.
  • Total Other Income, Net: $4.3 million, a decrease of 18.8% from the previous year, mainly due to a reduction in interest income.

Business Highlights

  • Product Development Pipeline: The company is focused on developing innovative therapies for immune-mediated and metabolic diseases, with key product candidates including reproxalap for dry eye disease and allergic conjunctivitis, and ADX-2191 for retinitis pigmentosa.
  • RASP Modulator Platform: Aldeyra is pioneering a novel pharmacologic approach with its RASP modulator platform, targeting reactive aldehyde species to modulate inflammation and other disease processes.
  • Clinical Trials and Results: The company has conducted multiple Phase 2 and Phase 3 clinical trials for its product candidates, demonstrating promising results in conditions such as dry eye disease, allergic conjunctivitis, psoriasis, asthma, COVID-19, and atopic dermatitis.
  • Regulatory Developments: The FDA has accepted a resubmitted NDA for reproxalap for dry eye disease, with a PDUFA date set for April 2, 2025. The company is also pursuing regulatory discussions for reproxalap in allergic conjunctivitis.
  • Partnerships and Collaborations: An exclusive option agreement with AbbVie has been established, granting AbbVie rights to develop and commercialize reproxalap, with potential milestone payments and profit-sharing arrangements.
  • Intellectual Property: The company holds a robust patent portfolio for its RASP modulators and ADX-2191, with patents granted in multiple countries and additional applications pending.
  • Manufacturing Strategy: The company relies on third-party contract manufacturers for production and has no plans to develop its own manufacturing facilities.
  • Future Outlook: Aldeyra plans to continue the development of its RASP modulator platform and expand its pipeline, with clinical trials for additional indications expected to commence in 2025.

Strategic Initiatives

  • Exclusive Option Agreement with AbbVie: The company entered into an exclusive option agreement with AbbVie Inc. to potentially collaborate on the development and commercialization of reproxalap, with AbbVie paying a non-refundable option fee and an extension fee. The agreement was amended to include launch activities and pre-commercialization planning.
  • Capital Management: Aldeyra has a Hercules Credit Facility, which was amended multiple times to extend the maturity date and interest-only payment period, and to adjust the interest rate. As of December 31, 2024, $15 million was outstanding under this facility. The company also entered into a sales agreement with Jefferies to offer and sell up to $75 million in common stock, although no sales had been made by the end of 2024. The company has not declared or paid any dividends and does not plan to do so in the foreseeable future, focusing instead on retaining funds for business operations.
  • Future Outlook: The company anticipates needing additional capital to fund the development and potential commercialization of its product candidates. It plans to raise funds through debt, equity, or partnerships and may adjust its clinical development plans as capital resources permit. The company expects to generate operating losses for the foreseeable future and is exploring opportunities for collaborations and partnerships to support its strategic initiatives.

Challenges and Risks

  • Regulatory Approval: The company faces significant risks related to the successful commercialization of reproxalap, which is crucial for its business. Delays or failure in obtaining FDA approval could severely impact revenue generation.
  • Capital Requirements: The company highlights the risk of not being able to raise additional capital if required, which could affect the development and commercialization of reproxalap and other product candidates.
  • Regulatory Compliance: Regulatory compliance is costly and time-consuming, and any changes in standards or requirements could lead to delays or prevent approvals.
  • Competition: Competition from other treatments that are approved more quickly or are more effective could reduce the company's commercial opportunities.
  • Operating Losses: The company has incurred significant operating losses since inception and expects to continue incurring losses, which raises concerns about achieving profitability.
  • Reliance on Third Parties: The reliance on third parties for clinical trials introduces risks of delays or non-compliance with regulations, potentially affecting the approval and commercialization timeline.
  • Public Health and Financial Services: Public health emergencies and adverse developments in the financial services industry could also impact business operations.
  • Market Risks: The company is exposed to market risks, including potential fluctuations in drug pricing and reimbursement levels, which could affect profitability. Legislative changes in healthcare policies could also impact the company's ability to secure favorable pricing and reimbursement for its products.
  • Manufacturing Risks: The company faces risks related to manufacturing, including potential disruptions and quality control issues, which could limit product supply and increase costs.

data.title: Update: Market Chatter: Verizon $2.4 Billion Contract Close to Be Nixed by FAA in Favor of Starlink
data.description:
The US Federal Aviation Administration is preparing to cancel a $2.4 billion communications overhaul contract currently awarded to Verizon Communications , The Washington Post reported late Wednesday, citing sources with knowledge of the matter.

The project is reportedly expected to be awarded to Elon Musk's Starlink.

The FAA and Starlink did not respond to a request for comment from MT Newswires.

In a statement, a Verizon spokesperson said Starlink boss Elon Musk mistakenly identified Verizon for its ongoing upgrade of the existing FAA communications system. Musk later issued a correction on his X account, writing, "the ancient system that is rapidly declining in capability was made [by] L3Harris (LHX)."

"To be clear, the FAA systems currently in place are run by L3Harris and not Verizon," the spokesperson said. "Our company is working on building the next generation system for the FAA which will support the Agency's mission for safe and secure air travel.

Verizon is about to start its multi-year contract and its solutions to replace the old systems is ready for deployment, the spokesperson added.

(Market Chatter news is derived from conversations with market professionals globally. This information is believed to be from reliable sources but may include rumor and speculation. Accuracy is not guaranteed.)

data.title: Middlesex Water Company Reports 2024 Financial Results
data.description:
• Diluted earnings per share (“EPS”) of $2.47 for 2024, compared to 2023 earnings of $1.76
  • Invested $75 million in capital expenditures in 2024 to upgrade, replace and enhance the resiliency of its water and wastewater utility infrastructure
  • Utility infrastructure investment of $387 million planned from 2025 through 2027

ISELIN, N.J., Feb. 28, 2025 (GLOBE NEWSWIRE) — Middlesex Water Company (“Middlesex” or the “Company”) today announced financial results for the fourth quarter and year end 2024. Middlesex reported net income of $44.4 million and diluted EPS of $2.47 for 2024, compared to net income of $31.5 million and diluted EPS of $1.76 in 2023.

"Our strong 2024 performance underscores the strength of our growth strategy, the dedication of our team members, a constructive regulatory environment, and the trust we have earned from our stakeholders,” said Nadine Leslie, President and Chief Executive Officer of Middlesex Water Company. “We are excited to build on this momentum to position the company for long-term growth while delivering life-sustaining water utility service at affordable rates to our customers.”

2024 Operating Results

Middlesex reported net income for 2024 of $44.4 million, a $12.8 million increase when compared to 2023. A significant amount of the increase is attributable to the base rate increase in the Middlesex System, one-time recovery of previously incurred depreciation, operating and carrying costs of $6.0 million (net of tax) associated with the prior year construction and operation of the water treatment facilities at Middlesex’s Park Avenue water treatment plant, and increases in weather-driven customer demand across all its operating regulated water utility systems.

Revenues for 2024 were $191.9 million, an increase of $25.6 million when compared to $166.3 million in 2023. Middlesex System revenues increased $19.9 million due primarily to the New Jersey Board of Public Utilities (“NJBPU”) approved base rate increase in March 2024, and increased weather-driven customer demand. In our Delaware System, Tidewater Utilities, Inc. (“Tidewater”) revenues increased $4.5 million due primarily to increased weather-related customer demand and customer growth. Non-regulated revenues increased $0.7 million due to higher supplemental contract services.

Operations and maintenance expenses for 2024, were $92.4 million, an increase of $9.3 million when compared to $83.1 million in 2023, primarily due to higher legal, financial, and regulatory matter costs, increased labor costs due to annual wage increases, higher costs related to an enhanced water treatment process at Middlesex’s Park Avenue water treatment plant, and higher energy costs due to increased water demand.

Fourth Quarter 2024 Operating Results

Middlesex reported net income of $8.8 million and diluted EPS of $0.49, for the fourth quarter of 2024, compared to net income of $5.8 million and diluted EPS of $0.32 for the same quarter in 2023. A significant amount of the increase is attributable to the base rate increase in the Middlesex System, increases in weather-driven customer demand across all its operating regulated water utility systems, and the implementation of new interim rates in Tidewater.

Infrastructure Investments

In 2024, Middlesex invested $75 million to upgrade, replace and enhance the resiliency of its water and wastewater utility infrastructure. These investments enhanced water quality, service reliability, security, and regulatory compliance.

In 2025, the Company plans to invest approximately $93 million in infrastructure investments, including our RENEW program to replace water mains in our Middlesex System, installation of hydrants and meters, and various water main and services replacements and improvements. From 2025 through 2027, the Company expects to invest $387 million in infrastructure investments, which includes $105 million to install treatment for Perfluoroalkyl (“PFAS”) at our Carl J. Olsen surface water treatment plant.

Selective and Sustainable Growth

In February 2025, the Delaware Public Service Commission approved the application of Tidewater to purchase the water utility assets of Ocean View for approximately $4.6 million. Ocean View serves approximately 900 customers in Sussex County, Delaware. The acquisition is expected to close by April 2025.

Quarterly Dividend Declared

On January 28, 2025, the Company’s Board of Directors declared a first quarter cash dividend of $0.34 per common share. Dividends on common shares will be paid on March 3, 2025, to shareholders of record as of February 18, 2025. The Company has paid cash dividends continually since 1912 and has increased the dividends received by shareholders each calendar year for 52 consecutive years.

About Middlesex Water Company

Middlesex Water Company is one of the nation’s premier investor-owned water and wastewater utilities. Established in 1897, Middlesex is a trusted provider of life-sustaining services to more than half a million people in New Jersey and Delaware. The company focuses on operational excellence, superior customer experience, investment in infrastructure, selective and sustainable growth, and employee engagement to deliver value to our customers, investors, and the communities we serve.

Forward-Looking Statements

This press release contains “forward‐looking statements” within the meaning of U.S. federal securities laws reflecting the current beliefs or expectations of Middlesex Water Company “MSEX” or the “Company” regarding its future performance, its financial condition, its strategic plans and cash flows, its results of operations as well as any other statements that do not directly relate to any historical or current facts. These statements reflect the Company’s current views and information currently available. This information is based on estimates, assumptions, and analysis that the Company believes, as of the date hereof, provide a reasonable basis for the information contained herein. data.description:
Toronto, Ontario--(Newsfile Corp. - February 28, 2025) - Lisa McDonald, Executive Director, Prospectors & Developers Association of Canada ("PDAC"), and her team joined Dean McPherson, Head, Business Development, Global Mining, Toronto Stock Exchange (TSX), to close the market to celebrate the opening of PDAC 2025.

Cannot view this video? Visit:

https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=cUdkOGp7im0

The Prospectors & Developers Association of Canada (PDAC) is the World's Premier Mineral Exploration and Mining Convention. The event, running March 2-5 at the Metro Toronto Convention Centre, will bring together thousands of participants from over 135 countries for its trade show, educational programming, networking events, business opportunities and fun. Since it began in 1932, the convention has grown in size, stature and influence. Today, it is the event of choice for the world's mineral industry hosting more than 1,100 exhibitors and 700 presenters.

PDAC is the leading voice of the mineral exploration and development community in Canada, an industry that employs more than 665,000 individuals, and contributed $125 billion to the nation's GDP in 2021. Currently representing over 8,000 members around the world, PDAC's work centers on supporting a competitive, responsible, and sustainable mineral sector.

data.title: Sitio Royalties Corp. Files 8K - Director, Officer or Compensation Filing >STR
data.description:
Sitio Royalties Corp. (STR) filed a Form 8K - Director, Officer or Compensation Filing - with the U.S Securities and Exchange Commission on February 28, 2025.

On February 27, 2025, Dawn Smajstrla ceased to serve as Chief Accounting Officer of Sitio Royalties Corp. (the "Company"). Ms. Smajstrla's departure is not the result of any disagreement with the Company on any matter relating to the Company's financials, operations, policies, or practices. Until a successor is appointed by the Company, Carrie Osicka, the Company's Chief Financial Officer, has assumed the responsibilities of the Company's principal accounting officer, effective as of February 27, 2025. Ms. Osicka's compensation will not change in connection with her increased responsibilities. Ms. Osicka's biography and other information required by Item 5.02(c) of Form 8-K are included in the Company's Schedule 14A filed with the SEC on March 29, 2024, and such information is incorporated herein by reference.

We intend to enter into a separation agreement with Ms. Smajstrla that will provide her with the separation benefits to which she is entitled under the Sitio Royalties Corp. Amended & Restated Severance Plan effective as of August 6, 2024, as amended (the "Severance Plan"), given that Ms. Smajstrla's cessation of service constituted a "Qualifying Termination" (as defined in the Severance Plan), subject to Ms. Smajstrla's executing and returning (and not revoking in the time provided to do so) a general release of claims. The Severance Plan was previously filed as Exhibit 10.5 to the Company's Quarterly Report on Form 10-Q for the quarterly period ended June 30, 2024.

The full text of this SEC filing can be retrieved at: https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1949543/000162828025009092/str-20250227.htm

Any exhibits and associated documents for this SEC filing can be retrieved at: https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1949543/000162828025009092/0001628280-25-009092-index.htm

data.title: Air France CEO says ready to bid for Portugal's TAP
data.description:
The CEO of Air France-KLM, Ben Smith, said on Friday the airline was ready to present its project for the privatisation of Portugal's flag carrier TAP, and intended to invest in local economy and connect the country's secondary cities.

The Franco-Dutch group, Lufthansa and British Airways owner IAG have already shown interest in TAP's privatisation, which the government wants to carry out this year.

"Air France-KLM is ready to present its project as part of TAP's privatisation process, a topic we have been following for a long time," Smith said in a statement during French President Emmanuel Macron's visit to Portugal.

Portugal's Prime Minister Luis Montenegro, who had previously insisted on a total privatisation of TAP, has signalled that a partial sale was a possibility.

The French president, speaking to Portuguese and French business leaders on Friday, said he "wants to find an innovative way to marry Air France and TAP".

Smith said Portugal is "a strategic market for Air France-KLM" and TAP is an "emblematic airline that has built a powerful hub in Lisbon, open to the world."

TAP's most attractive assets are its key slots to Brazil, Portuguese-speaking African countries and the U.S. from its Lisbon hub, which the Portuguese government wants to maintain.

"We want to maintain and develop TAP's strong brand and highly valuable hub, drawing on the expertise of the company's staff."

Smith said his groups wants "to strengthen the connectivity of the entire country, including secondary cities, and invest durably in the local economy".

Air France-KLM said last month it was exploring potential cooperation on sustainable aviation fuel with Portuguese partners ahead of the privatisation.
